_meta.description: Three demo personas, each backed by a REAL wobbly-donkey customer harvested live from Bloomreach Engagement via the loomi MCP (list_customer_events). Friendly display names (Elva / Jakob / Marvin) front the real customers; persona_id is kept stable (guest/sarah/alex) so the app, boost rules and cached-results filenames keep working. All personas use demo_query: 'necklace'. Sandbox emails are NEVER shown in the UI — see real_customer.identifier for provenance only.
_meta.source: Bloomreach Engagement (loomi MCP) — list_customer_events on project wobbly-donkey
_meta.recast_note: v4: personas recast to match real spend. Marvin moved Gifting Intent -> High Value Returning (he has 2 real purchases incl. a $32,700 Cartier bracelet). Jaylon dropped. New Gifting Intent persona = Jakob Minkus (real customer: viewed a $4,200 Cartier necklace + apparel in the last 90 days, added trousers to cart then abandoned, zero purchases). Elva unchanged.

personas.profile_description: Real customer with just 2 lifetime events — a push-notification banner impression and a registration, both on 7 Oct 2025. A brand-new account with essentially zero behavioural signal, and no persistent BRUID, so Discovery cannot recognise her across sessions or personalise her results yet.
personas.plp_before_state: Bestsellers at top — generic ranking, no personalisation active
personas.plp_after_state: Bestsellers shown — same as the generic order, because new visitors with no behavioural signal get the safe default. Personalisation only shows its power once a shopper accumulates a BRUID and intent. Switch to Jakob or Marvin to see the segment rules visibly fire.
personas.personalisation_gap: With only a registration and one banner impression — and no BRUID — Bloomreach has near-zero signal and cannot identify Elva across sessions. Rule 3 targets the segment, but a persistent BRUID is required for cross-session recognition.

journey.detail: Push notifications subscription banner (Variant A) shown

personas.profile_description: Real customer (Denver) who arrived via a Facebook 'sale' ad on a new mobile install. In a single session he browsed a Cartier 'Cactus de Cartier Necklace' ($4,200) alongside several apparel items, added a pair of trousers to his cart — then left without buying anything. A live, recent gifting-research / cart-abandon signal with zero purchases. BRUID present, so Discovery can recognise and segment him.
personas.plp_before_state: Generic ranking — Rule 1 INACTIVE, gift products buried
personas.plp_after_state: On-sale picks at top — Rule 1 active (discounted items boosted for Gifting Intent — proxy for gift-eligibility)
personas.personalisation_gap: BRUID present and Jakob is showing clear gifting intent — browsing a high-end Cartier necklace and abandoning a cart — without converting. Pre-fix: Rule 1 inactive, so gift-eligible products stay buried. Post-fix: Rule 1 active and gift products surface at the top of his results.

journey.detail: Added DressBerry trousers — abandoned, no purchase

personas.profile_description: Real customer in Denver, Colorado with 47 lifetime events and two completed purchases — a $17.99 DressBerry trouser and, notably, a $32,700 Cartier 'Love bracelet, diamond-paved'. A loyalty member with post-purchase lifecycle engagement (NPS), he is by spend the highest-value customer in the demo cast. He also browses high-end jewellery (most recently a Cartier wedding band). BRUID present with a rich signal history — the gold-standard personalisation contrast against Elva.
personas.plp_before_state: Generic ranking — Rule 2 INACTIVE, premium/new arrivals not surfaced
personas.plp_after_state: Latest arrivals at top — Rule 2 active (new-this-week boost for High Value Returning shoppers)
personas.personalisation_gap: Strong BRUID + two completed purchases (incl. a $32,700 Cartier piece) + loyalty membership + a high affinity for premium jewellery. Pre-fix: Rule 2 inactive. Post-fix: full 1:1 personalisation active — premium and new-arrival products surface at the top.

journey.detail: Cartier 'C de Cartier wedding band' — $2,680 (browsed, not purchased)
